metadata.author: Utimaco - support@utimaco.com
metadata.comments: Solution template for Utimaco Enterprise Secure Key Manager

metadata.description: Not used, but needed to pass arm-ttk test `Location-Should-Not-Be-Hardcoded`.  We instead use the `workspace-location` which is derived from the LA workspace
[concat('Region to deploy solution resources -- separate from location selection',parameters('location'))]
Workspace name for Log Analytics where Microsoft Sentinel is setup
resource group name where Microsoft Sentinel is setup
subscription id where Microsoft Sentinel is setup

connectorUiConfig.title: Utimaco Enterprise Secure Key Manager (ESKM)
connectorUiConfig.descriptionMarkdown:
The Utimaco ESKM connector ingests KMIP server logs from Utimaco Enterprise Secure Key Manager (ESKM) into Microsoft Sentinel.

Supports Basic authentication (username/password).

For more information, visit [Utimaco ESKM Documentation](https://utimaco.com/key-management/enterprise-secure-key-manager).

graphQueries.baseQuery: {{graphQueriesTableName}} | where TimeGenerated > ago(14d) | summarize count() by bin(TimeGenerated, 1d)

sampleQueries.description: All KMIP server events from the last 24 hours

sampleQueries.query: {{graphQueriesTableName}} | summarize count() by Event | order by count_ desc

sampleQueries.description: Failed KMIP Client Operations with reason codes
sampleQueries.query: {{graphQueriesTableName}} | where Result != 'SUCCESS' | summarize count() by Operation, Result, Reason | order by count_ desc

sampleQueries.description: Authentication failures by client IP and user
sampleQueries.query: {{graphQueriesTableName}} | where Event == 'Authentication Failure' | summarize FailureCount = count() by IP, User | order by FailureCount desc
{{graphQueriesTableName}} | where isnotempty(Operation) | summarize count() by Operation, ObjectType | order by count_ desc

resourceProvider.permissionsDisplayText: read and write permissions are required.

customs.name: Utimaco ESKM Account Credentials
customs.description: Utimaco ESKM account credentials (username and password) are required to authenticate to the ESKM management API. See [Utimaco ESKM Documentation](https://utimaco.com/key-management/enterprise-secure-key-manager).

instructionSteps.title: Step 1 - Prerequisites
instructionSteps.description: Ensure you have a Utimaco ESKM management account with permissions to access the management API. Note the base URL of your ESKM appliance (typically `https://<eskm-host>:8443`).

instructionSteps.title: Step 2 - Connect to Utimaco ESKM
instructionSteps.description: Provide your Utimaco ESKM management API base URL and account credentials below, then click Connect.

dataFlows.transformKql: source | extend TimeGenerated = todatetime(['ts']) | project TimeGenerated, Action = tostring(['action']), Event = tostring(['event']), Message = tostring(['message']), IP = tostring(['ip']), User = tostring(['user']), UUID = tostring(['uuid']), Operation = tostring(['operation']), ObjectType = tostring(['objectType']), Result = tostring(['result']), Reason = tostring(['reason'])

columns.name: Action
columns.description: Subsystem or component generating the log (e.g., KMIP Server)

columns.name: Event
columns.description: Event type (e.g., StateChange, Authentication Failure, Authentication Success, ClientOperation)

columns.name: IP
columns.description: Client IP address associated with the event

columns.name: User
columns.description: KMIP user account associated with the operation

columns.name: UUID
columns.description: Unique identifier of the KMIP managed object

columns.name: Operation
columns.description: KMIP operation performed (e.g., CREATE, DESTROY, REGISTER, ACTIVATE, REVOKE, SIGN, GET, LOCATE, CERTIFY, ADD_ATTRIBUTE)

columns.name: ObjectType
columns.description: Type of KMIP object (e.g., SYMMETRIC_KEY, PUBLIC_KEY, PRIVATE_KEY, CERTIFICATE)

columns.name: Reason
columns.description: Reason code when operation fails (e.g., PERMISSION_DENIED, ITEM_NOT_FOUND)

metadata.description: Visualize Utimaco ESKM KMIP server events ingested into Microsoft Sentinel.

properties.serializedData:
{"version":"Notebook/1.0","items":[{"type":1,"name":"title","content":{"json":"# 🔐 Utimaco ESKM — KMIP Monitoring
---
Key security signals from your Utimaco Enterprise Secure Key Manager: activity baseline, event distribution, operation outcomes and authentication health.

Use the **Time Range** filter to scope every chart on this page."}},{"type":9,"name":"filters","content":{"version":"KqlParameterItem/1.0","parameters":[{"id":"e234304e-41d6-4139-944d-baba8c4da127","version":"KqlParameterItem/1.0","name":"time_range","label":"Time Range","type":4,"isRequired":true,"value":{"durationMs":604800000},"typeSettings":{"selectableValues":[{"durationMs":3600000},{"durationMs":14400000},{"durationMs":43200000},{"durationMs":86400000},{"durationMs":172800000},{"durationMs":604800000},{"durationMs":1209600000},{"durationMs":2419200000}],"allowCustom":true}}],"style":"pills","title":"Filters"}},{"type":1,"name":"section-overview","content":{"json":"### 📊 Overview"}},{"type":3,"name":"kpi-tiles","content":{"version":"KqlItem/1.0","query":"let data = UtimacoESKMKmipServerLogs_CL | where TimeGenerated {time_range};
union
  (data | summarize Value = tolong(count()) | extend Metric = 'Total events', Sort = 1),
  (data | summarize Value = tolong(dcount(User)) | extend Metric = 'Unique users', Sort = 2),
  (data | summarize Value = tolong(dcount(IP)) | extend Metric = 'Unique source IPs', Sort = 3),
  (data | summarize Total = count(), Failed = countif(isnotempty(Result) and Result != 'SUCCESS') | extend Value = tolong(iff(Total == 0, 0, toint(round(100.0 * Failed / Total, 0)))) | extend Metric = 'Failure %', Sort = 4 | project Metric, Value, Sort)
| order by Sort asc
| project Metric, Value","size":3,"queryType":0,"resourceType":"microsoft.operationalinsights/workspaces","timeContextFromParameter":"time_range","visualization":"tiles","tileSettings":{"titleContent":{"columnMatch":"Metric","formatter":1},"leftContent":{"columnMatch":"Value","formatter":12,"formatOptions":{"palette":"auto"}},"showBorder":true}}},{"type":1,"name":"section-activity-mix","content":{"json":"### 📈 Activity Mix"}},{"type":3,"name":"event-distribution","content":{"version":"KqlItem/1.0","query":"UtimacoESKMKmipServerLogs_CL
| where TimeGenerated {time_range}
| where isnotempty(Event)
| summarize Count = count() by Event
| order by Count desc","size":0,"queryType":0,"resourceType":"microsoft.operationalinsights/workspaces","timeContextFromParameter":"time_range","title":"Event distribution","visualization":"barchart"},"customWidth":"50"},{"type":3,"name":"top-kmip-operations","content":{"version":"KqlItem/1.0","query":"UtimacoESKMKmipServerLogs_CL
| where TimeGenerated {time_range}
| where isnotempty(Operation)
| summarize Count = count() by Operation
| order by Count desc
| take 10","size":0,"queryType":0,"resourceType":"microsoft.operationalinsights/workspaces","timeContextFromParameter":"time_range","title":"Top KMIP operations","visualization":"barchart"},"customWidth":"50"},{"type":1,"name":"section-outcomes","content":{"json":"### ✅ KMIP Operation Outcomes"}},{"type":3,"name":"operation-results","content":{"version":"KqlItem/1.0","query":"UtimacoESKMKmipServerLogs_CL
| where TimeGenerated {time_range}
| where isnotempty(Operation) and isnotempty(Result)
| extend ResultCategory = iif(Result == 'SUCCESS', 'Success', 'Failure')
| summarize Count = count() by ResultCategory
| order by Count desc","size":0,"queryType":0,"resourceType":"microsoft.operationalinsights/workspaces","timeContextFromParameter":"time_range","title":"Success vs Failure","visualization":"piechart"},"customWidth":"50"},{"type":3,"name":"failed-operations","content":{"version":"KqlItem/1.0","query":"UtimacoESKMKmipServerLogs_CL
| where TimeGenerated {time_range}
| where isnotempty(Result) and Result != 'SUCCESS'
| summarize Count = count() by Operation
| order by Count desc
| take 10","size":0,"queryType":0,"resourceType":"microsoft.operationalinsights/workspaces","timeContextFromParameter":"time_range","title":"Failed operations breakdown","visualization":"barchart"},"customWidth":"50"},{"type":1,"name":"section-auth","content":{"json":"### 🔑 Authentication Activity"}},{"type":3,"name":"auth-trend","content":{"version":"KqlItem/1.0","query":"UtimacoESKMKmipServerLogs_CL
| where TimeGenerated {time_range}
| where Event in ('Authentication Success', 'Authentication Failure')
| summarize Count = count() by bin(TimeGenerated, 15m), Event
| order by TimeGenerated asc","size":0,"queryType":0,"resourceType":"microsoft.operationalinsights/workspaces","timeContextFromParameter":"time_range","title":"Authentication success vs failure trend","visualization":"timechart","chartSettings":{"createOtherGroup":0,"seriesLabelSettings":[{"seriesName":"Authentication Success","color":"green"},{"seriesName":"Authentication Failure","color":"redBright"}]}}},{"type":1,"name":"section-timeline","content":{"json":"### ⏱ Activity Timeline"}},{"type":3,"name":"activity-trend","content":{"version":"KqlItem/1.0","query":"UtimacoESKMKmipServerLogs_CL
| where TimeGenerated {time_range}
| summarize Count = count() by bin(TimeGenerated, 15m)
| order by TimeGenerated asc","size":0,"queryType":0,"resourceType":"microsoft.operationalinsights/workspaces","timeContextFromParameter":"time_range","title":"KMIP activity over time","visualization":"timechart"}}],"fromTemplateId":"sentinel-UserWorkbook","$schema":"https://github.com/Microsoft/Application-Insights-Workbooks/blob/master/schema/workbook.json"}

properties.description: @{workbookKey=UtimacoESKMWorkbook; logoFileName=Azure_Sentinel.svg; description=Visualize Utimaco ESKM KMIP server events ingested into Microsoft Sentinel.; dataTypesDependencies=System.Object[]; dataConnectorsDependencies=System.Object[]; previewImagesFileNames=System.Object[]; version=1.0.0; title=Utimaco ESKM KMIP Events; templateRelativePath=ESKMworkbook.json; subtitle=; provider=Utimaco}.description
UtimacoESKM_RareKmipUsers_HuntingQueries Hunting Query with template version 3.0.0

properties.displayName: Utimaco ESKM - Rare KMIP users in the last 24 hours
properties.query:
let lookback = 14d;
let recent = 24h;
let historical = UtimacoESKMKmipServerLogs_CL
    | where TimeGenerated between (ago(lookback) .. ago(recent))
    | where isnotempty(User)
    | summarize by User;
UtimacoESKMKmipServerLogs_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(recent)
| where isnotempty(User)
| summarize EventCount = count(), FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated), LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated), SourceIPs = make_set(IP, 25), Operations = make_set(Operation, 50) by User
| join kind=leftanti (historical) on User
| extend AccountCustomEntity = User

tags.name: description
tags.value: Surfaces KMIP user accounts seen on Utimaco ESKM within the last 24 hours that were NOT observed in the prior 14 days. New or rarely-seen accounts may indicate a newly provisioned credential, lateral movement, or an unauthorized integration.

properties.description: Utimaco Enterprise Secure Key Manager Hunting Query 1

properties.displayName: Utimaco ESKM - Rare KMIP users in the last 24 hours

properties.description: UtimacoESKM_NewSourceIPs_HuntingQueries Hunting Query with template version 3.0.0

properties.displayName: Utimaco ESKM - New source IPs connecting to KMIP
properties.query:
let lookback = 14d;
let recent = 24h;
let historical = UtimacoESKMKmipServerLogs_CL
    | where TimeGenerated between (ago(lookback) .. ago(recent))
    | where isnotempty(IP)
    | summarize by IP;
UtimacoESKMKmipServerLogs_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(recent)
| where isnotempty(IP)
| summarize EventCount = count(), FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated), LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated), Users = make_set(User, 25), Operations = make_set(Operation, 50) by IP
| join kind=leftanti (historical) on IP
| extend IPCustomEntity = IP

tags.name: description
tags.value: Identifies client IPs connecting to Utimaco ESKM in the last 24 hours that were not observed in the prior 14 days. New source IPs against a key management appliance can indicate a newly onboarded service, a misconfigured client, or an unauthorized application reaching cryptographic material.

properties.description: Utimaco Enterprise Secure Key Manager Hunting Query 2

properties.displayName: Utimaco ESKM - New source IPs connecting to KMIP
Utimaco ESKM - High-volume private key retrievals by user
properties.query:
UtimacoESKMKmipServerLogs_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(24h)
| where Operation in ("GET", "EXPORT")
| where ObjectType in ("PRIVATE_KEY", "SYMMETRIC_KEY")
| where Result == "SUCCESS"
| where isnotempty(User)
| summarize RetrievalCount = count(), DistinctObjects = dcount(UUID), FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated), LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated), SourceIPs = make_set(IP, 25), ObjectTypes = make_set(ObjectType, 10), SampleUUIDs = make_set(UUID, 50) by User
| where RetrievalCount >= 25 or DistinctObjects >= 10
| order by RetrievalCount desc
| extend AccountCustomEntity = User

tags.name: description
tags.value: Surfaces users issuing an unusually high number of KMIP GET / EXPORT operations against PRIVATE_KEY objects on Utimaco ESKM over the last 24 hours. Bulk retrieval of private keys may indicate credential harvesting or pre-exfiltration staging of cryptographic material.

properties.description: Utimaco Enterprise Secure Key Manager Hunting Query 3

properties.displayName: Utimaco ESKM - High-volume private key retrievals by user

properties.query:
let businessStartHourUTC = 6;
let businessEndHourUTC = 20;
UtimacoESKMKmipServerLogs_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(7d)
| where isnotempty(Operation)
| extend HourOfDay = datetime_part("Hour", TimeGenerated), DayOfWeek = dayofweek(TimeGenerated)
| where HourOfDay < businessStartHourUTC or HourOfDay >= businessEndHourUTC or DayOfWeek == 0d or DayOfWeek == 6d
| summarize EventCount = count(), Operations = make_set(Operation, 50), ObjectTypes = make_set(ObjectType, 25), FirstSeen = min(TimeGenerated), LastSeen = max(TimeGenerated), SourceIPs = make_set(IP, 25) by User
| order by EventCount desc
| extend AccountCustomEntity = User

tags.name: description
tags.value: Surfaces KMIP operations performed against Utimaco ESKM outside typical business hours (configurable; defaults to 20:00-06:00 UTC and weekends). Useful for hunting opportunistic or insider activity that deliberately avoids working-hours monitoring. Tune the time window and weekday filter for your environment.

properties.description: Utimaco Enterprise Secure Key Manager Hunting Query 4

Detects 5 or more KMIP "Authentication Failure" events originating from the same client IP within a 15 minute window on Utimaco ESKM. May indicate brute-force or credential-stuffing activity against the KMIP server.
properties.displayName: Utimaco ESKM - Multiple KMIP authentication failures from same IP
properties.query:
UtimacoESKMKmipServerLogs_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(15m)
| where Event == "Authentication Failure"
| where isnotempty(IP)
| summarize FailureCount = count(), StartTime = min(TimeGenerated), EndTime = max(TimeGenerated), Users = make_set(User, 50) by IP
| where FailureCount >= 5
| extend IPCustomEntity = IP

properties.description: Utimaco Enterprise Secure Key Manager Analytics Rule 1

properties.displayName: Utimaco ESKM - Multiple KMIP authentication failures from same IP

properties.description: Detects a single KMIP user receiving 10 or more PERMISSION_DENIED responses within 30 minutes on Utimaco ESKM. May indicate privilege probing, key access reconnaissance, or a misconfigured application.
properties.displayName: Utimaco ESKM - PERMISSION_DENIED burst for a KMIP user
properties.query:
UtimacoESKMKmipServerLogs_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(30m)
| where Reason == "PERMISSION_DENIED"
| where isnotempty(User)
| summarize DeniedCount = count(), StartTime = min(TimeGenerated), EndTime = max(TimeGenerated), Operations = make_set(Operation, 50), ObjectTypes = make_set(ObjectType, 25), SourceIPs = make_set(IP, 25) by User
| where DeniedCount >= 10
| extend AccountCustomEntity = User

properties.description: Utimaco Enterprise Secure Key Manager Analytics Rule 2

properties.displayName: Utimaco ESKM - PERMISSION_DENIED burst for a KMIP user

properties.description: UtimacoESKM_DestroyBurst_AnalyticalRules Analytics Rule with template version 3.0.0

Detects a single KMIP user issuing 20 or more DESTROY operations within 10 minutes on Utimaco ESKM. May indicate mass key/object deletion, insider sabotage, or ransomware-style cryptographic-material destruction.
properties.displayName: Utimaco ESKM - Burst of KMIP DESTROY operations by a single user
properties.query:
UtimacoESKMKmipServerLogs_CL
| where TimeGenerated > ago(10m)
| where Operation == "DESTROY"
| where Result == "SUCCESS"
| where isnotempty(User)
| summarize DestroyCount = count(), StartTime = min(TimeGenerated), EndTime = max(TimeGenerated), ObjectTypes = make_set(ObjectType, 25), UUIDs = make_set(UUID, 100), SourceIPs = make_set(IP, 25) by User
| where DestroyCount >= 20
| extend AccountCustomEntity = User

properties.description: Utimaco Enterprise Secure Key Manager Analytics Rule 3

properties.displayName: Utimaco ESKM - Burst of KMIP DESTROY operations by a single user

properties.descriptionHtml:
Note: Please refer to the following before installing the solution:

• Review the solution Release Notes

• There may be known issues pertaining to this Solution, please refer to them before installing.

The Utimaco ESKM solution ingests KMIP server logs from Utimaco Enterprise Secure Key Manager (ESKM) into Microsoft Sentinel using the Codeless Connector Platform (CCP). It enables monitoring of key management operations, authentication events, and KMIP client activity, helping you detect misuse, configuration issues, and unauthorized access to cryptographic material.

Underlying Microsoft Technologies used:

  1. Microsoft Sentinel Codeless Connector Platform (CCP)

  2. Azure Monitor Data Collection Rules and Endpoints

Data Connectors: 1, Workbooks: 1, Analytic Rules: 3, Hunting Queries: 4

Learn more about Microsoft Sentinel | Learn more about Solutions
